entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: The geographic region where the small-scale communal irrigation schemes are located

entities.name: SMALL-SCALE COMMUNAL IRRIGATION SCHEMES
entities.description: Irrigation systems managed by smallholder farmers in communal settings, often under-performing

entities.name: SOCIO-ECOLOGICAL SYSTEMS
entities.description: Complex systems that integrate social and ecological components, which irrigation schemes are recognized as

entities.name: SUBSISTENCE ORIENTATION
entities.description: A production focus aimed at survival rather than commercial profit

entities.name: POOR MARKET INTEGRATION
entities.description: The lack of effective connection between small-scale producers and commercial markets

entities.name: LOW INVESTMENT CAPACITY
entities.description: The limited ability of farmers to invest financial resources into crop production

entities.name: LOW YIELDS
entities.description: Reduced agricultural productivity per unit of land

entities.name: PAYMENT DIFFICULTIES
entities.description: Challenges faced by farmers in paying for water usage

entities.name: LACK OF MAINTENANCE PARTICIPATION
entities.description: The unwillingness or inability of users to participate in the upkeep of the irrigation system

entities.name: UNSUSTAINABLE RESOURCE UTILIZATION
entities.description: The use of water and land in a manner that cannot be maintained over the long term

entities.name: FAILED INFRASTRUCTURE
entities.description: Broken or decaying physical components of the irrigation system

entities.name: INEFFICIENT RESOURCE USE
entities.description: The ineffective application and management of water and land resources

entities.name: RESOURCE CONFLICT
entities.description: Increased disputes among users over access to water and land

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL IRRIGATION SCHEME DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: A traditional approach to developing irrigation systems that prioritizes technical infrastructure

entities.name: HARD TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Physical infrastructure and technical tools used to improve irrigation functionality

entities.name: INFRASTRUCTURE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The level of functionality and performance of the physical irrigation components

entities.name: IRRIGATION APPLICATION TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Specific technical methods used to apply water to crops

entities.name: FARMER LIVELIHOODS
entities.description: The overall well-being and economic security of irrigation farmers

entities.name: PROFITABILITY
entities.description: The capacity of the farming system to generate financial returns

entities.name: INFRASTRUCTURE REHABILITATION
entities.description: The process of repairing and restoring decayed irrigation infrastructure, often subsidized by donors
